mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment exceeding 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS monthly growth exceeds 150,000, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in real retail and food service sales below the prior 3-month trend signals weakening consumer demand that compresses near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS declines > 1.5% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises above 5.5%, signaling a supply-side contraction coupled with labor market weakness that reduces equity valuations and forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure or post-shock recovery weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where both conditions are met simultaneously, or joint Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST exceed 1,200 thousand units annualized while real retail sales growth remains positive, signaling sustained consumer and construction demand that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1,200 thousand units AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0%
regime_hint: expansion phase with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 58% of instances where both conditions hold, or joint Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rises above 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, signaling real monetary accommodation that erodes bond yields and redirects capital to equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both conditions are met, or joint Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and corporate hiring expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.4 percentage points in 3 months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 62% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates to below 0.2% month-over-month while real GDP growth remains below 2.0% annualized, signaling a supply-demand mismatch in a low-growth regime that compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth < 0.2% AND GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0%
regime_hint: low-growth or stagnation regime with supply weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances where both conditions hold, or joint Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
